body: We audited 50 mid-market manufacturing lines last month. The biggest margin killer wasn't labor costs or raw material spikes. It was 'ghost inventory'—parts marked as 'in-stock' that physically weren't there, causing 4.2 hours of average weekly downtime. You don't need more staff; you need a better map of your warehouse. Stop guessing, start measuring.

Most supply chain software is built for the boardroom, not the floor. If your shift leads spend 20 minutes every morning just logging into a dashboard to see what's delayed, your software is failing you. We built Harbor to be 'glanceable.' Five seconds to see the bottleneck, ten seconds to re-route. Tech should save time, not demand it.

The 'Just-in-Time' era isn't dead, it's just getting smarter. The old way: keep 20% safety stock and pray. The Harbor way: use real-time transit data to keep 5% safety stock and know exactly when to trigger the next order. One of our clients just freed up $240k in working capital by trusting the data over their gut. Math beats intuition every time.
